claims.claim: AI x crypto / decentralized AI: native economic rails for AI agents, machine-to-machine payments, verifiable compute/inference, agents as economic actors
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Stablecoins & onchain payments are crypto's clearest product-market fit, onboarding hundreds of millions
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: DePIN — token incentives bootstrap real-world networks for compute/GPUs, energy, etc.
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Decentralized social & onchain identity — user ownership of social graph, proof-of-personhood
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Concerned about memecoins / pure speculation as extractive casino dynamics
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Concerned about decentralization theater / re-centralization undermining credible neutrality
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Risk-on: long-term/multi-year structural optimism, funding builders through every cycle, near-term froth acknowledged
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Strong new emphasis on privacy as crypto's key moat — private money and programmable private computation (encrypted smart contracts) for institutions
Heavy emphasis on onchain capital markets / tokenization of traditional assets — perps, prediction markets, onchain lending/credit, RWAs (Arc/Circle $75M investment)
Active policy advocacy for specific legislation (CLARITY bill, GENIUS Act praise)
Raised a16z Crypto Fund 5 ($2.2B) explicitly to back builders during low-sentiment period — concrete action on the risk-on thesis
Framing of a pragmatic, non-ideological 'collared shirt era' of crypto founders
Strong emphasis on AI driving software productivity and AI coding tools (Codex vs Claude) — adjacent personal conviction
notes: The core infrastructure theses (AI x crypto, stablecoins, risk-on long-termism) are strongly and repeatedly confirmed, including the concrete action of raising a $2.2B fund into low sentiment. DePIN is confirmed via references to onchain capital markets for compute/energy. Decentralization theater concern is confirmed through extended defenses of credible neutrality vs re-centralization. Decentralized social/identity and the memecoin concern were not addressed in the window (absent). The biggest unanticipated theme is privacy as the central moat, plus a pronounced focus on onchain capital markets/tokenization and direct legislative advocacy.
